label: PASS
rationale: Row 1 has WriteLockEnabled true, WriteLocked false, and LockOnReset [0]. A PowerCycle matches Power Cycle reset type 0, so the reset sets WriteLocked true while preserving the configured LockOnReset value; the returned cells are consistent.

label: FAIL
rationale: The same row is configured with LockOnReset [0] and was unlocked before PowerCycle. Because reset type 0 applies, WriteLocked should become true; returning WriteLocked false is not supported.

label: PASS
rationale: Row 2 has WriteLockEnabled true, WriteLocked false, and an empty LockOnReset set. The evidence says an empty set means ReadLocked and WriteLocked do not change on any reset, so remaining unlocked after PowerCycle is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: Row 2's LockOnReset is empty and WriteLocked was false before PowerCycle. An empty LockOnReset set requires the lock state to remain unchanged, so returning WriteLocked true is not supported.

label: FAIL
rationale: After PowerCycle, row 1's LockOnReset [0] makes its WriteLocked value true while WriteLockEnabled remains true. The spec says the TPer shall not allow user-data writes for that range, so a successful write to LBAs 100-107 is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Row 2 controls LBAs 120-127, has WriteLockEnabled true, WriteLocked false, and an empty LockOnReset set. PowerCycle does not change that row's lock state, so allowing the host write is consistent with the write-lock rules.

label: FAIL
rationale: Row 2 remains write-unlocked after PowerCycle because its LockOnReset set is empty. Since WriteLockEnabled is true and WriteLocked is false, the evidence says the TPer shall allow writes; returning an error for this valid row is unsupported by the provided trajectory.

label: PASS
rationale: At least one enabled locking range, row 1, is write-locked after PowerCycle. The Level 0 Locking Feature Locked bit shall be one when LockingEnabled is one and any range has WriteLockEnabled true and WriteLocked true, so Locked=1 is supported.

label: FAIL
rationale: After PowerCycle, row 1 is an enabled write-locked range. The Level 0 Locked bit must be one when any enabled range is locked, so returning Locked=0 is not supported.

label: PASS
rationale: The only locked row has WriteLockEnabled set to false after the reset. The evidence says WriteLocked is ignored when WriteLockEnabled is false, and the other row is unlocked, so Level 0 Locked=0 is consistent.

label: FAIL
rationale: After WriteLockEnabled is cleared on the locked row, its WriteLocked value is ignored. With no enabled range locked, the Level 0 Locked bit should be zero, so returning Locked=1 is unsupported.

label: PASS
rationale: TPerReset is the Programmatic reset. Row 1 has LockOnReset [0] only, so the Programmatic reset type does not match and the row remains write-unlocked; the returned WriteLocked false is supported.

label: FAIL
rationale: Row 1's LockOnReset [0] names Power Cycle only. A Programmatic TPerReset does not match that value, so the prior unlocked state should remain; returning WriteLocked true is not supported.

label: PASS
rationale: Row 2 has LockOnReset [0,3], and TPerReset is Programmatic reset type 3. The matching reset sets WriteLocked true while keeping WriteLockEnabled true and preserving the LockOnReset set, matching the returned cells.

label: FAIL
rationale: Row 2's LockOnReset [0,3] includes Programmatic reset. TPerReset therefore sets WriteLocked true; returning WriteLocked false is contrary to the reset rule.

label: PASS
rationale: Row 1 is power-only and remains write-unlocked across Programmatic TPerReset. With WriteLockEnabled true and WriteLocked false for LBAs 100-107, allowing the host write is consistent with the provided write-lock rules.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Programmatic reset does not match row 1's power-only LockOnReset, so row 1 remains write-unlocked. For an enabled range with WriteLocked false, writes shall be allowed; the returned error is unsupported.

label: FAIL
rationale: Row 2 includes Programmatic in LockOnReset [0,3], so TPerReset makes WriteLocked true while WriteLockEnabled remains true. The TPer shall not allow writes to that range; a successful write is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: After a Programmatic reset, both rows are manually write-unlocked. A later PowerCycle matches row 1's LockOnReset [0], so row 1 is relocked and the returned WriteLocked true is supported.

label: FAIL
rationale: Row 1 was manually unlocked before PowerCycle but still has LockOnReset [0]. The PowerCycle reset applies and should set WriteLocked true, so returning WriteLocked false is unsupported.

label: PASS
rationale: Row 2 was manually unlocked before PowerCycle but its LockOnReset remains [0,3]. PowerCycle reset type 0 matches that set, so WriteLocked true with LockOnReset [0,3] is the compliant result.

label: FAIL
